implementation_difficulty_reason: The existing Delta F = 2 neutral-meson machinery already covers D0 mixing with VLL/VRR/LR operator support and a D0-specific evaluator; catalog updates mainly require source/value refresh and sidecar provenance.

required_grep_commands: rg -n "epsilon_K|epsilon_k|Delta m_K|DELTA_M_K|evaluate_delta_mk|B_d|b_d|B_s|b_s|D0|d_mix|mu.?e.?gamma|muToEGamma|BR_LIMIT|MEG" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'

rg -n "b.?->.?s|X_s|K\\*|R_K|R_D|B_s.*mu|pi.*nu|nu.*nubar|epsilon.?prime|eps.?prime|t.?->|Z.?->|h.?->|EDM|dipole|conversion|tau.?->|mu.?->.?3e" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'

rg -n "D0|D\\^0|D mixing|d_mix|Delta_M_D|DELTA_M_D|DELTA_M_D_EXP|evaluate_d0_mixing|x_D|y_D|delta_y" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'
evidence: quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:252 defines the default D-mixing DeltaF2Input.
quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:256 wires the D-mixing reject reason to d_mix.
quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:655 starts the D0 hadronic-parameter block.
quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:665 sets DELTA_M_D_EXP = 6.25e-15 GeV from HFLAV.
quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:949 uses the D0 budget in the evaluator.
quarkConstraints/scan.py:103 includes d_mix_ratio in scan output columns.
quarkConstraints/scan.py:439 records d_mix_ratio from the D-system DeltaF2 result.
quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:23 includes D0 in the modern policy surface.
quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:46 labels D0 as conservative non-CP mixing.
quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:1356 evaluates D0 mixing from the QCD-evolved bridge.
quarkConstraints/modern/inputs.py:991 defines the modern D0 neutral-meson input.

sections.process:
\textbf{Standard notation:} neutral-charm mixing, parameterized by
\(x_D=\Delta m_D/\Gamma_D\), \(y_D=\Delta\Gamma_D/(2\Gamma_D)\), and
\(\Delta m_D=|m_1-m_2|\).  This entry covers the CP-even mixing magnitudes
used for RS-flavor constraints; the dedicated CP-violation observables
\(|q/p|\) and \(\phi_D\) belong to C002.
sections.pdg_prose:
The headline values are the HFLAV CKM25 all-CPV global-fit results, version
through 14 September 2025 (\texttt{hflav\_ckm25\_dmixing\_global\_fit}):
\[
  x_D=(0.405\pm0.043)\%,\qquad
  y_D=(0.636\pm0.024)\%.
\]
The 95\% C.L. intervals are \(x_D\in[0.320,0.489]\%\) and
\(y_D\in[0.590,0.682]\%\).  These values are traced to
\texttt{flavor\_catalog/references/C001/hflav\_ckm25\_dmixing\_global\_fit.txt}
and to the PDG 2025 Table 70.7 excerpt.  PDG Live separately gives the
absolute mass difference (\texttt{pdg2025\_deltam\_d\_pdgLive}),
\[
  \Delta m_D=(0.997\pm0.116)\times10^{10}\hbar\,{\rm s}^{-1}
             =(6.56\pm0.76)\times10^{-15}\,{\rm GeV},
\]
from \texttt{flavor\_catalog/references/C001/pdg2025\_deltam\_d\_pdgLive.txt}.
For the orchestrator-requested \(\delta y\) block, the sidecar records the
latest HFLAV/LHCb CPV-sensitive input
\(\Delta y=(0.031\pm0.035\pm0.013)\%\)
(\texttt{hflav\_ckm25\_delta\_y\_input}); it also records the derived
\(\Delta\Gamma_D/\Gamma_D=2y_D=(1.272\pm0.048)\%\)
(\texttt{hflav\_ckm25\_dmixing\_global\_fit}).
sections.relevance:
In anarchic warped models, KK-gluon and other heavy neutral exchanges generate
\(\Delta C=2\) four-quark operators connecting \(u\) and \(c\).  The same
operator-basis logic used for \(K\), \(B_d\), and \(B_s\) mixing applies, but
the charm system is up-sector and long-distance dominated.  It is therefore a
useful complementary test of whether the scan's flavor alignment suppresses
both down- and up-sector neutral currents.
sections.post_2008:
The CFW 2008 baseline (\texttt{cfw2008\_arxiv0804\_1954}) already treated
warped anarchic flavor as strongly constrained, with generic RS KK-gluon
scales around \(21\) TeV and composite pseudo-Goldstone scenarios around
\(33\) TeV.  Since then, the charm-mixing experimental picture has become much
sharper.  LHCb's 2021 \(D^0\to K_S^0\pi^+\pi^-\) analysis
(\texttt{lhcb2021\_arxiv2106\_03744}) observed a nonzero neutral-charm mass
difference, and the current HFLAV/PDG global fits decisively exclude the
no-mixing point.  The same HFLAV fit remains consistent with no indirect CP
violation, so the CP-even magnitudes are the clean C001 inputs.
sections.validity:
As a new-physics constraint this is robust in the sense that any short-distance
\(\Delta C=2\) amplitude contributes to \(M_{12}^D\).  It is also
SM-theory-limited: the observed mixing is believed to be dominated by
long-distance Standard Model amplitudes that are hard to compute.  The safe
catalog interpretation is therefore conservative, bounding the magnitude of a
new-physics contribution rather than subtracting a precise SM prediction.

In down-aligned or kaon-protected RS variants, up-sector observables become
leading rather than secondary diagnostics.
sections.code_coverage_prose:
\textbf{YES.}  The legacy lane has a default D-mixing input at
\texttt{quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:252}, with reject reason
\texttt{d\_mix} at \texttt{quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:256}, D0 hadronic inputs
starting at \texttt{quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:655}, and
\texttt{DELTA\_M\_D\_EXP} at \texttt{quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:665}.  The
numeric evaluator is \texttt{evaluate\_d0\_mixing} at
\texttt{quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:941}.  The scan writes
\texttt{d\_mix\_ratio} at \texttt{quarkConstraints/scan.py:103} and
\texttt{quarkConstraints/scan.py:439}.  The modern policy surface also lists
\(D^0\) at \texttt{quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:23} and evaluates
the bridge result at \texttt{quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:1356}.
sections.implementation_difficulty_prose:
\textbf{LOW.}  The existing \(\Delta F=2\) machinery already contains the
D0 evaluator and the required VLL/VRR/LR operator structure.  A future
production update would mainly refresh the experimental value and document the
conservative long-distance treatment, not add a new operator basis.
sections.key_references:
Process-local source keys before bibliography consolidation:
\texttt{hflav\_ckm25\_dmixing\_global\_fit},
\texttt{pdg2025\_deltam\_d\_pdgLive},
\texttt{pdg2025\_dmixing\_review\_table70\_7},
\texttt{lhcb2021\_arxiv2106\_03744}, and
\texttt{cfw2008\_arxiv0804\_1954}.

L15: - Reports stronger bounds around 33 TeV in the composite pseudo-Goldstone case.
L16: - Concludes that fully anarchic warped flavor is strongly constrained and may
L17:   require partial flavor symmetries.
